title: # Start Kernel Orchestration Start Kernel orchestration for the current task inside this Codex session. Execute immediately. Do not create or edit GitHub issues, pull requests, review comments, or issue comments. Do not inspect CI unless explicitly asked later. Backup-only GitHub Actions dispatch or...
summary_text: 1. **High**: CI still does not execute the live runtime smoke path, so the real canary failure mode is untested in automation. Evidence: gate workflow only runs static contract script (`bash scripts/check-codex-kernel-prompt.sh`) without `RUN_CODEX_KERNEL_SMOKE=1` in [fugue-orchestration-gate.yml](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator/.github/workflows/fugue-orchestration-gate.yml):168 and [fugue-orchestration-gate.yml](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator/.github/workflows/fugue-orchestration-gate.yml):171. Risk: single-lane/degraded runtime behavior can pass CI and only fail in manual real-canary runs. 2. **High**: `timeout-audit` uses `threads.source='exec'` + marker-title filtering, but tests around rollout DB fixtures still mostly insert `source='cli'`; this selector boundary remains unproven end-to-end. Evidence: query in [check-codex-kernel-prompt.sh](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator/scripts/check-codex-kernel-prompt.sh):174 vs fixture inserts in [test_watch.py](/Users/masayuki/Dev/tools/codex-kernel-guard/tests/test_watch.py):83 and [test_launcher_integration.py](/Users/masayuki/Dev/tools/codex-kernel-guard/tests/test_launcher_integration.py):252 and [test_kernel_guard_integration.py](/Users/masayuki/Dev/tools/codex-prompt-launcher/tests/test_kernel_guard_integration.py):205. Risk: real sessions not matching the exact source/title shape intermittently bypass timeout audit. 3. **High**: toolchain tests that matter for real canary (`tools/codex-prompt-launcher`, `tools/codex-kernel-guard`) are outside the fugue repo test jobs and are not wired into the repository CI path. Evidence: no workflow/test references to those suites from fugue tests/workflows (search hit only backup script mention). Risk: local adapter/guard regressions are invisible to repo CI despite being in the runtime critical path. 4. **Medium**: `thread_hint + success-on-quorum` branches are implemented but not explicitly covered by integration tests. Evidence: runtime uses thread hint + quorum overrides in [cli.py](/Users/masayuki/Dev/tools/codex-kernel-guard/src/codex_kernel_guard/cli.py):532, [cli.py](/Users/masayuki/Dev/tools/codex-kernel-guard/src/codex_kernel_guard/cli.py):711, [session_watch.py](/Users/masayuki/Dev/tools/codex-kernel-guard/src/codex_kernel_guard/session_watch.py):523, [session_watch.py](/Users/masayuki/Dev/tools/codex-kernel-guard/src/codex_kernel_guard/session_watch.py):711, but there are no corresponding test cases toggling these env/paths. Risk: convergence behavior added for real canary can drift without test detection. 5. **Medium**: bootstrap-evidence strict validation (trusted root, freshness window, prompt_root match, required providers) is security-correct but currently untested as a matrix. Evidence: validation logic in [cli.py](/Users/masayuki/Dev/tools/codex-kernel-guard/src/codex_kernel_guard/cli.py):62-118, no dedicated tests covering stale timestamp / prompt-root mismatch / provider-missing branches. Risk: benign timing/path variations can surface as `audit: FAIL` and appear as canary instability. Open questions I would resolve next: 1. In the failing host, does the newest canary thread row consistently have `source='exec'` and marker in `title`? 2. Are the `tools/*` test suites being executed anywhere in your current integration pipeline, or only manually? 3. During failures, is the path hitting fresh-session branch or timeout-audit branch (`can_try_timeout_audit`) in [check-codex-kernel-prompt.sh](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator/scripts/check-codex-kernel-prompt.sh):283-305?
